reference_alignment.assessment: Current mesh-native case uses AVL/VSPAERO-style full-wing Sref and AVL Cref for SU2 REF_AREA/REF_LENGTH. Sampled VSP span and area are slightly lower because the faceted station surface samples the VSP geometry; this is a provenance note, not evidence of force normalization failure.

force_direction_check.assessment: At AOA=0 and V=(6.5,0,0), SU2 CD is aligned with the positive freestream/x direction and CL with z for this case.

marker_audit.assessment: Only wing_wall is assigned as no-slip wall and only wing_wall is in MARKER_MONITORING. Farfield is not monitored for force.

force_breakdown.assessment: No unexpected marker force contribution was observed. Total force equals the only monitored surface, wing_wall.

engineering_notes: The current long-run CD is positive after convergence; it is not the earlier negative-short-transient issue.
The force audit proves marker ownership and coefficient direction. It does not prove final aerodynamic fidelity or grid independence.

The computed CL is still far below the VSPAERO panel reference, so next physics work should focus on grid refinement, half-wing/symmetry cost reduction, viscous/turbulence/transition modeling, and geometry incidence/reference provenance rather than force-marker leakage.
